---
title: "SynthDiD.jl Introduction"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
    self-contained: true
jupyter: julia-1.12
execute:
  cache: false
---

## Overview

SynthDiD.jl is a Julia implementation of the Synthetic Difference-in-Differences
estimator proposed by Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens & Wager (2021).
It implements three estimators:

- **Synthetic Diff-in-Diff (SDiD)**: Optimizes both unit weights $\omega$ and time weights $\lambda$
- **Synthetic Control (SC)**: Optimizes unit weights $\omega$ only, with $\lambda = 0$
- **Diff-in-Diff (DiD)**: Uses uniform weights $\omega = 1/N_0$, $\lambda = 1/T_0$

## Setup

```{julia}
using SynthDiD
using Plots
using DataFrames
using Random
using Printf
Random.seed!(12345)
```

## California Proposition 99

We estimate the effect of California Proposition 99 (a 1988 tobacco tax) on
per-capita cigarette consumption.

```{julia}
df = california_prop99()
first(df, 5)
```

Convert the long panel to matrix format:

```{julia}
setup = panel_matrices(df, :State, :Year, :PacksPerCapita, :treated)
years = collect(setup.times)
println("Y: $(size(setup.Y)),  N₀ = $(setup.N0),  T₀ = $(setup.T0)")
```

## Point Estimates

```{julia}
τ_sdid = synthdid_estimate(setup.Y, setup.N0, setup.T0;
                           units=setup.units, times=years)
τ_sc   = sc_estimate(setup.Y, setup.N0, setup.T0;
                     units=setup.units, times=years)
τ_did  = did_estimate(setup.Y, setup.N0, setup.T0;
                      units=setup.units, times=years)

println(τ_sdid)
println(τ_sc)
println(τ_did)
```

### Comparing estimates

```{julia}
println("─" ^ 50)
@printf("%-30s %10s\n", "Estimator", "τ̂")
println("─" ^ 50)
for (name, est) in [("Diff-in-Diff", τ_did), ("Synthetic Control", τ_sc), ("Synthetic Diff-in-Diff", τ_sdid)]
    @printf("%-30s %10.2f\n", name, est.estimate)
end
println("─" ^ 50)
```

## Standard Errors

We have only one treated unit (California), so the only applicable variance
estimation method is the **placebo** method (Algorithm 4 of Arkhangelsky et al.).
Bootstrap and jackknife require multiple treated units.

```{julia}
Random.seed!(12345)
se_val = se(τ_sdid; method=:placebo, replications=200)
@printf("Point estimate: %1.2f\n", τ_sdid.estimate)
@printf("Placebo SE:     %1.2f\n", se_val)
@printf("95%% CI:         (%1.2f, %1.2f)\n",
        τ_sdid.estimate - 1.96 * se_val,
        τ_sdid.estimate + 1.96 * se_val)
```

## Parallel Trends Plot

The built-in plot recipe shows treated vs. synthetic control trajectories,
with a vertical line at treatment onset and time weight bars below.

```{julia}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
plot(τ_sdid)
```

## Comparing All Three Estimators

Pass a vector of estimates to get a side-by-side comparison:

```{julia}
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 4
plot([τ_did, τ_sc, τ_sdid])
```

## Customized Parallel Trends

We can also build custom plots using the package's accessors.
This replicates the style of Figure 1 in Arkhangelsky et al.

```{julia}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5

Y = setup.Y
years = setup.times
T0 = setup.T0
N0 = setup.N0

ω_sdid = τ_sdid.weights.omega
synth_sdid = vec(ω_sdid' * Y[1:N0, :])
california = vec(Y[N0+1, :])

p = plot(years, synth_sdid;
    label="Synthetic Control", color=:steelblue, linewidth=2,
    xlabel="Year", ylabel="Packs per Capita",
    title="California vs. Synthetic Control (SDiD)",
    legend=:topright, size=(700, 400))
plot!(p, years, california;
    label="California", color=:firebrick, linewidth=2)
vline!(p, [years[T0] + 0.5]; label="", color=:gray40, linestyle=:dash)

# Shade post-treatment gap
post_yrs = years[(T0+1):end]
plot!(p, post_yrs, synth_sdid[(T0+1):end];
    fillrange=california[(T0+1):end], fillalpha=0.15,
    fillcolor=:firebrick, label="", color=:transparent)
p
```

## Effect Curve

The per-period treatment effects averaged to produce the SDiD estimate:

```{julia}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4
curve = effect_curve(τ_sdid)
post_years = years[(T0+1):end]

bar(post_years, curve;
    label="Per-period effect", color=:steelblue, alpha=0.7,
    xlabel="Year", ylabel="Treatment Effect",
    title="Per-Period Treatment Effect (SDiD)",
    size=(700, 350))
hline!([τ_sdid.estimate]; label="Average τ̂ = $(round(τ_sdid.estimate, digits=1))",
    color=:firebrick, linestyle=:dash, linewidth=2)
hline!([0]; label="", color=:gray50, linewidth=0.5)
```

## Weights

### Unit Weights ($\omega$)

The SDiD unit weights show which control states contribute most:

```{julia}
ω = τ_sdid.weights.omega
control_names = setup.units[1:N0]
top_idx = sortperm(ω, rev=true)[1:10]

println("Top 10 Control Weights (SDiD):")
println("─" ^ 40)
for i in top_idx
    @printf("  %-20s  %0.4f\n", control_names[i], ω[i])
end
@printf("\n  Effective N₀ = %0.1f\n", 1 / sum(ω .^ 2))
```

```{julia}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4
# Top 15 control weights as horizontal bar chart
top15 = sortperm(ω, rev=true)[1:15]
bar(ω[top15], orientation=:h,
    yticks=(1:15, string.(control_names[top15])),
    label="", color=:steelblue, alpha=0.8,
    xlabel="Weight ω", title="Top 15 Unit Weights (SDiD)",
    size=(700, 400), yflip=true)
```

### Time Weights ($\lambda$)

```{julia}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.5
λ = τ_sdid.weights.lambda

bar(years[1:T0], λ;
    label="", color=:steelblue, alpha=0.7,
    xlabel="Year", ylabel="Weight λ",
    title="Time Weights (SDiD)", size=(700, 300))
vline!([years[T0] + 0.5]; label="Treatment onset", color=:firebrick, linestyle=:dash)
```

### Weight Summary Table

The `synthdid_controls` function returns the top control weights across estimators:

```{julia}
synthdid_controls([τ_sdid, τ_sc, τ_did]; weight_type="omega", mass=0.9)
```

## Placebo Test

We run a placebo estimate using only pre-treatment data:

```{julia}
τ_placebo = placebo(τ_sdid)
@printf("Actual estimate:  %1.2f\n", τ_sdid.estimate)
@printf("Placebo estimate: %1.2f\n", τ_placebo.estimate)
```

The placebo estimate should be close to zero if the parallel trends assumption
holds in the pre-treatment period.

## Method

The SDiD estimator solves a weighted TWFE regression:

$$
\hat\tau^{sdid} = \arg\min_{\tau,\mu,\alpha,\beta} \sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{t=1}^T
(Y_{it} - \mu - \alpha_i - \beta_t - W_{it}\tau)^2 \hat\omega_i \hat\lambda_t
$$

where the weights $\hat\omega$ and $\hat\lambda$ are estimated via Frank-Wolfe
optimization on the unit simplex with ridge regularization.

| Estimator | Unit weights $\omega$ | Time weights $\lambda$ | Intercept |
|-----------|----------------------|------------------------|-----------|
| SC        | Optimized            | $\lambda = 0$          | No        |
| DiD       | $1/N_0$ (uniform)    | $1/T_0$ (uniform)      | Yes       |
| SDiD      | Optimized            | Optimized              | Yes       |

## Reference

Arkhangelsky, D., Athey, S., Hirshberg, D. A., Imbens, G. W., & Wager, S. (2021).
Synthetic Difference-in-Differences.
*American Economic Review*, 111(12), 4088-4118.
